summary: Fixed tooling edge cases in `tools/wiki_tu.py` so translation-unit wiki table generation handles missing assignee columns and partial assignee metadata independently. The script now guards against short table rows before reading Discord/GitHub columns, stores only present assignee fields, and emits Discord and GitHub placeholders independently when either field is absent.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; updated wiki translation-unit table parsing and output formatting for assignee metadata edge cases.

what_changed: In `read_tr`, replaced direct indexing of `td[5]` and `td[6]` with `len(td) > 5` and `len(td) > 6` checks before reading Discord and GitHub assignee columns.

Changed assignee construction from always storing both `discord` and `github` keys when either column was present to building an `assignee` dict containing only the fields that actually exist and are non-empty.
Changed output generation to default `assignees.get(file, {})` and evaluate Discord and GitHub fields separately.
Discord output now falls back to `<!-- Discord -->` when no Discord assignee is present, without requiring GitHub to also be absent.
GitHub output now falls back to `<!-- GitHub -->` when no GitHub assignee is present, without requiring Discord to also be absent.
smart_moves: Avoided `IndexError` by checking row length before accessing optional wiki table columns.
Made the assignee model sparse: only present fields are stored, which better represents partially populated table rows.
Separated Discord and GitHub rendering paths so one missing field does not suppress or break the other field.
Preserved existing placeholder comments for missing Discord/GitHub cells, maintaining table structure and likely downstream wiki formatting expectations.
decomp_lessons: Repository tooling that consumes wiki-maintained tables should tolerate incomplete or older row formats instead of assuming every optional column exists.
For contributor/assignee metadata, treat Discord and GitHub handles as independent optional fields; do not require both to be present to render either one.
When generating status tables for decomp progress, use placeholders for absent metadata to keep stable column output while avoiding bogus links or crashes.
Small robustness fixes in scripts like `wiki_tu.py` can reduce friction for maintaining translation-unit progress pages.
naming_conventions: Assignee dictionary keys are literal lowercase strings: `discord` and `github`.
Missing table-cell placeholders use HTML comments with capitalized labels: `<!-- Discord -->` and `<!-- GitHub -->`.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Find other tooling scripts that parse wiki tables with fixed column indexes and may need `len(row)` guards.
Check whether generated TU wiki output remains stable when only Discord or only GitHub assignee metadata is present.
Search for other uses of `assignees.get(file)` that assume both `discord` and `github` keys exist.
